care.summary: A striking variegated snake plant with tall, sword-shaped leaves edged in yellow. Extremely low-maintenance and drought-tolerant.
care.light: Bright indirect to low light. Variegation is best maintained in bright indirect light; too little light may cause the yellow margins to fade.
care.water: Allow soil to dry completely between waterings. Water sparingly, every 2-6 weeks depending on season and light. Overwatering is the most common cause of failure.
care.soil: Well-draining cactus or succulent mix. Add perlite or coarse sand to improve drainage.
care.humidity: Average household humidity is fine; tolerates dry air.
care.fertilizer: Feed a balanced liquid fertilizer diluted to half strength once a month during spring and summer. Do not fertilize in winter.
care.toxicity: Mildly toxic if ingested; keep away from pets and children. May cause nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
common_issues: Root rot from overwatering (yellowing, mushy leaves)
Fading variegation due to insufficient light
Leaf curling or browning tips from underwatering or low humidity
tips: Use a pot with drainage holes and avoid letting the plant sit in water.
Wipe leaves occasionally to remove dust and keep pores open.
Rotate the plant every few months for even growth.
